methodology.anchors: 90-100 market leader · 70-89 strong/production-ready · 50-69 usable but gappy · 30-49 weak/indirect · 0-29 unsupported. Community is log-scale (~16M npm/week → 95; 100k → 70; 1k → 35; <500 → 15-25). CLI scenario favors installable tools; libraries are scored on suitability for building one. Unmeasured performance scores are architecture-based estimates.
methodology.provenance: Competitor criterion scores carried from 2026-07-20 panel unless material product change (Vercel AI SDK harnesses/sandbox/WorkflowAgent; Mastra Workspace + AgentBrowser + CodeMode + E2B). Deuz re-scored from scratch against local 1.8.0 autonomy surface (workspace, compute/CodeAct, planTasks/verifyStep, runtime, browser, ./providers, ./testing, azure/bedrock). Community numbers re-fetched live 2026-07-22 (npm downloads API + GitHub API).

criteria.coding: F78 D86 P78 C95 E80 $70 — ToolLoopAgent + experimental_sandbox; harness adapters (Claude Code/Codex/Pi); still not a first-party coding-agent product
criteria.asi: F88 D86 P78 C95 E88 $72 — WorkflowAgent durable steps + needsApproval; OPA policy package; ecosystem gravity

scores.name: Mastra
scores.delta: +2.8 vs 2026-07-20 — Workspace + AgentBrowser + CodeMode + E2B/Daytona sandboxes

criteria.chatbot: F76 D74 P60 C80 E82 $82 — agent layer strong; chat UI still rides AI SDK
criteria.cli: F68 D74 P58 C80 E82 $80 — no CLI product; heavy Node deps (~116 MB)
criteria.coding: F82 D72 P62 C80 E80 $80 — workspace fs/shell, CodeMode, AgentBrowser a11y refs, remote sandboxes
criteria.asi: F88 D76 P62 C80 E84 $78 — workflow snapshots, approvals, workspace persistence, CostGuard
criteria.agi: F78 D74 P58 C80 E86 $80 — 600+ model router; memory/RAG packages; browser + sandbox breadth

criteria.chatbot: F90 D78 P86 C23 E88 $92 — 1.7 wire/resume/approvals + 1.8 plan-update/activity/verify parts through useChat; azure/bedrock/providers breadth. Missing: Vue/Svelte, component catalog, speech.
criteria.cli: F78 D74 P84 C23 E86 $90 — still no CLI product; runtime + workspace + CodeAct + browser are the building kit for one. Node reference sandbox is not production isolation.
criteria.coding: F78 D72 P84 C23 E80 $88 — biggest 1.8 jump: createWorkspaceTools, codeActTool/shellTool, createNodeSandbox, createBrowserTools/Playwright. Gaps: no first-party git toolset; sandbox is host process not E2B/Docker.
criteria.asi: F94 D74 P85 C23 E92 $86 — planTasks → verifyStep → bestOfN/selfConsistency/parallelAgents; RunManager + pollStaleRuns; Workspace externalized memory; signed approvals + durable checkpoints retained from 1.7.
criteria.agi: F78 D74 P86 C23 E90 $90 — published ./providers registry + azure + bedrock (Mantle); browser + parallelAgents. Still no speech/realtime/swarm product.

notes: Self-assessed by the Deuz maintainers — rubric, criterion breakdowns and sources published for scrutiny; re-derive any score from the anchors.
Deuz community criterion is ONE number (23) applied to every scenario: 393 npm downloads/week + 2 GitHub stars, fetched live 2026-07-22.
Deuz is scored against the unreleased 1.8.0 code surface in this repo (exports present; npm still 1.7.1). That is intentional — the benchmark tracks shipped code, not marketing version numbers.

Honesty check: Mastra's remote sandboxes (E2B/Daytona) and AgentBrowser a11y-ref tooling outrank Deuz's Node reference sandbox on production isolation. Deuz wins on zero-deps + edge-safe seams + verifyStep-in-loop.

Sources 2026-07-22: mastra.ai/docs/workspace, mastra.ai/docs/browser/agent-browser, ai-sdk.dev WorkflowAgent + experimental_sandbox + harness packages, Deuz packages/core/src/{autonomy,workspace,compute,browser,runtime,providers,testing,azure,bedrock}.ts
